title: Smoky Molten Venezuelan Tofu Grill
description: A smoky, molten Venezuelan dish featuring tofu, robata-grill, and jain-friendly ingredients. This dish is perfect for a sick day when you need a comforting and flavorful meal.
instructions: Preheat your robata grill to medium-high heat.
Cut the tofu into 1-inch cubes and season with smoked paprika, cumin, coriander, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper.
Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a pan over medium heat and saut1e the tofu until golden brown on all sides.
In a blender or food processor, combine chipotle peppers in adobo sauce, sugar, lime juice, and 1 tablespoon of olive oil. Blend until smooth.
Pour the chipotle sauce over the tofu and toss to coat.
Thread the tofu and roasted red peppers onto the robata grill.
Grill the tofu and peppers for 2-3 minutes per side, or until the tofu is golden brown and the peppers are tender.
Serve the grilled tofu and peppers in a quinoa or corn tortilla with chopped fresh cilantro and a squeeze of lime juice.
Warm the tortillas by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
To assemble the dish, place a portion of grilled tofu and peppers onto a tortilla, followed by a sprinkle of cilantro and a squeeze of lime juice.
